goals.text: Provide a local command that validates whether a requirement directory is ready for write-plan.
Return deterministic diagnostics for missing files, invalid JSON, invalid structure, and acceptance coverage gaps.
Add focused pytest coverage for valid and invalid requirement directories.

rules.text: The validator must not mutate requirement files or call Linear tools.
The validator must check all required write-plan input files.
The validator must parse contract.json and acceptance.json as JSON.
Each acceptance item must reference at least one source requirement or rule.
The command must exit 0 only when no diagnostics with severity error are present.

entities.name: Requirement Directory
entities.description: A directory under .vibeRig/requirements/<requirement-id>/ containing local Docs as Code planning files.

entities.name: Diagnostic
entities.description: A deterministic validation result with code, severity, file path, and message.

entities.name: Acceptance Item
entities.description: A structured acceptance criterion consumed by write-plan and referenced by Linear tasks.

workflows.name: Validate one requirement directory
steps: Resolve the target requirement directory from a CLI argument.
Parse contract and acceptance JSON files.
Validate structural fields needed by write-plan.
Exit with 0 when there are no error diagnostics, otherwise exit non-zero.

constraints.text: Use the existing scripts/ and tests/ layout.
Keep implementation scoped to local validation.
Avoid adding mandatory third-party runtime dependencies.

risks.text: Structural validation may diverge from schema files if future requirement contracts change.
A command that is too broad may become another local task system, which is out of scope.

decisions.text: Implement the first version as a local preflight command only.
Use stable diagnostic codes to make both tests and agent consumption reliable.
